```{r phase-9-export-setup}
  item = c("Figure directory", "Table directory", "Interactive directory"),
  path = c(figure_dir, table_dir, interactive_dir),
  status = c(
    "Created",
    "Created",
    ifelse(interactive_exports_enabled, "Created", "Created for optional HTML exports")
  )
)

knitr::kable(export_setup, caption = "Phase 9 export folders")
```

```{r phase-9-validation-plot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=7}
selected_validation_point <- validation_table[k == selected_k]
validation_subtitle <- if (phase6_validation_mode == "consensus_sampled") {
  paste(
    "Consensus silhouette validation across",
    phase6_validation_repeats,
    "repeats of",
    validation_rows,
    "rows"
  )
} else if (phase6_validation_mode == "sampled") {
  paste("Sampled silhouette validation on", validation_rows, "rows")
} else {
  "Full-data silhouette validation"
}

validation_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  validation_table,
  ggplot2::aes(x = k, y = silhouette)
) +
  # v2.0: 0.30 threshold band so reader sees where results sit
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
    ymin = 0.30, ymax = Inf, alpha = 0.06, fill = "#31a354"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
    ymin = -Inf, ymax = 0.30, alpha = 0.06, fill = "#fb6a4a"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.30, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#e31a1c", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text", x = max(validation_table$k),
    y = 0.305, label = "Threshold 0.30",
    hjust = 1, size = 2.9, color = "#e31a1c"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(color = "#1f78b4", linewidth = 0.9) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(color = "#1f78b4", size = 2.6) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(
    xintercept = selected_k, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#33a02c", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(
    data = selected_validation_point,
    color = "#33a02c", size = 4.5
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(
    ggplot2::aes(label = round(silhouette, 3)),
    nudge_y = max(validation_table$silhouette) * 0.03,
    size = 3.2, color = "#1f78b4"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "label",
    x = selected_k,
    y = selected_validation_point$silhouette[[1]],
    label = paste0("Selected k = ", selected_k,
                   "\nSilhouette = ",
                   round(selected_validation_point$silhouette[[1]], 3)),
    fill   = "#e5f5e0", color = "#006d2c",
    label.size = 0.25, alpha = 0.95, size = 3
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Silhouette Validation Index by k",
    subtitle = validation_subtitle,
    x        = "Number of clusters (k)",
    y        = "Silhouette width"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )

print(validation_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_validation_index_plot.png"),
  plot = validation_plot,
  width = 7,
  height = 4.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-stability-plot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=7}
stability_plot_data <- data.table::melt(
  copy(stability_summary),
  id.vars = "k",
  measure.vars = c("rand", "jaccard"),
  variable.name = "metric",
  value.name = "score"
)

# v2.0: Quality-aware subtitle — explicitly flags red-zone result
stability_quality_label <- if (!sil_pass || !jaccard_pass) {
  paste0(
    "WARNING: EXPLORATORY results. ",
    "Jaccard = ", round(selected_jaccard_v2, 3),
    " (threshold 0.40). ",
    "Silhouette = ", round(selected_silhouette, 3),
    " (threshold 0.30)."
  )
} else {
  paste0(
    "Cluster quality meets thresholds. ",
    "Jaccard = ", round(selected_jaccard_v2, 3),
    ", Silhouette = ", round(selected_silhouette, 3), "."
  )
}

stability_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  stability_plot_data,
  ggplot2::aes(x = factor(k), y = score, fill = metric)
) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
    ymin = 0.75, ymax = 1.05, alpha = 0.08, fill = "#31a354"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
    ymin = 0.60, ymax = 0.75, alpha = 0.08, fill = "#fec44f"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "rect", xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
    ymin = 0.00, ymax = 0.60, alpha = 0.06, fill = "#fb6a4a"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(
    ggplot2::aes(label = round(score, 3)),
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.65),
    vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.40, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#e31a1c", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.60, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#fd8d3c", linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.75, linetype = "dotted",
    color = "#238b45", linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = 0.41,
    label = "Jaccard threshold 0.40",
    hjust = 1.05, size = 2.8, color = "#e31a1c"
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text", x = Inf, y = 0.76,
    label = "Stable threshold 0.75",
    hjust = 1.05, size = 2.8, color = "#238b45"
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("rand" = "#4292c6", "jaccard" = "#e6550d"),
    labels = c("rand" = "Rand index", "jaccard" = "Jaccard index")
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Cluster Stability — Rand and Jaccard by k",
    subtitle = stability_quality_label,
    x        = "Number of clusters (k)",
    y        = "Stability score",
    fill     = "Metric"
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1.05)) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title    = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(
      color = if (!sil_pass || !jaccard_pass) "#cb181d" else "#238b45",
      size  = 9
    ),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )

print(stability_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_stability_plot.png"),
  plot = stability_plot,
  width = 7,
  height = 4.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-spatial-scenario-heatmap, fig.height=5.5, fig.width=8}
heatmap_data <- merge(
  spatial_scenario_crosstab,
  scenario_final_names[, .(scenario_cluster, scenario_name_short = wrap_label(final_name, width = 18))],
  by = "scenario_cluster",
  all.x = TRUE
)

heatmap_data <- merge(
  heatmap_data,
  spatial_final_names[, .(spatial_cluster, spatial_name_short = wrap_label(final_name, width = 20))],
  by = "spatial_cluster",
  all.x = TRUE
)

heatmap_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  heatmap_data,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = factor(scenario_cluster),
    y = factor(spatial_cluster),
    fill = spatial_cluster_pct
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white") +
  ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = round(spatial_cluster_pct, 1)), size = 3) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient(low = "#deebf7", high = "#08519c") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Phase 9 Spatial-Scenario Cross-Tab Heatmap",
    x = "Scenario cluster",
    y = "Spatial cluster",
    fill = "% within\nspatial cluster"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )

print(heatmap_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap.png"),
  plot = heatmap_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-spatial-cluster-facets, fig.height=7, fig.width=9}
top_spatial_clusters <- non_noise_assignments[order(-cluster_size)][1:min(.N, 6), spatial_cluster]

spatial_cluster_heatmap_data <- merge(
  df_spatial_plot[spatial_cluster %in% top_spatial_clusters],
  spatial_final_names[, .(spatial_cluster, final_name)],
  by = "spatial_cluster",
  all.x = TRUE
)

spatial_cluster_heatmap_data[, final_name_short := wrap_label(final_name, width = 26)]

spatial_cluster_facet_heatmap <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  spatial_cluster_heatmap_data,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x = x_km,
    y = y_km,
    fill = after_stat(log10(count + 1))
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_bin2d(bins = 50) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_viridis_c(
    option = "D",
    name = "log10(bin\ncount + 1)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::coord_equal() +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ final_name_short, ncol = 2) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Phase 9 Spatial Cluster Heatmaps",
    subtitle = "Each panel shows the internal density shape of one of the largest hotspot clusters",
    x = "Projected x (km)",
    y = "Projected y (km)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(spatial_cluster_facet_heatmap)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_spatial_cluster_facets_heatmap.png"),
  plot = spatial_cluster_facet_heatmap,
  width = 9,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-scenario-risk-bubble, fig.height=5, fig.width=7.5}
dataset_sev34_pct <- round(100 * mean(as.integer(df_clustered$Severity) %in% c(3, 4)), 2)

risk_bubble_data <- merge(
  scenario_severity_profile,
  scenario_weather_time_profile[, .(
    scenario_cluster,
    dominant_weather_group,
    dominant_hour_band,
    mean_visibility
  )],
  by = "scenario_cluster",
  all.x = TRUE
)

risk_bubble_data <- merge(
  risk_bubble_data,
  scenario_final_names[, .(scenario_cluster, final_name)],
  by = "scenario_cluster",
  all.x = TRUE
)

# v2.0: Use truncated final name as label; add sev4_vs_baseline annotation
risk_bubble_data[, short_label := paste0(
  "S", scenario_cluster, ": ",
  substr(gsub(" ---.*", "", final_name), 1, 18)
)]
risk_bubble_data <- merge(
  risk_bubble_data,
  scenario_severity_profile[, .(scenario_cluster, sev4_vs_baseline)],
  by = "scenario_cluster", all.x = TRUE
)

risk_bubble_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  risk_bubble_data,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x     = n,
    y     = sev34_pct,
    size  = pmax(sev4_pct, 0.1),
    color = dominant_weather_group
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(
    yintercept = dataset_sev34_pct, linetype = "dashed",
    color = "#e31a1c", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  ggplot2::annotate(
    "text", x = -Inf, y = dataset_sev34_pct + 0.3,
    label = paste("Dataset baseline:", dataset_sev34_pct, "%"),
    hjust = -0.05, size = 2.9, color = "#e31a1c"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.82) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    ggplot2::aes(label = short_label),
    size = 3, show.legend = FALSE,
    box.padding = 0.4, max.overlaps = 10
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 14)) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Scenario Cluster Risk and Size Profile",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Bubble size = Sev4 share. Dashed line = dataset Sev3/4 baseline (",
      dataset_sev34_pct, "%). Label shows cluster name prefix."
    ),
    x     = "Cluster size (n)",
    y     = "Severity 3/4 share (%)",
    size  = "Sev4 share (%)",
    color = "Weather group"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "right"
  )

print(risk_bubble_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_scenario_risk_bubble.png"),
  plot = risk_bubble_plot,
  width = 7.5,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-county-composition, fig.height=4.8, fig.width=7.5}
county_plot_data <- data.table::melt(
  copy(scenario_county_profile),
  id.vars = c("scenario_cluster", "n", "dominant_county"),
  measure.vars = c("Nassau_pct", "Suffolk_pct", "Queens_pct"),
  variable.name = "county",
  value.name = "pct"
)

county_plot_data[, county := factor(
  county,
  levels = c("Nassau_pct", "Suffolk_pct", "Queens_pct"),
  labels = c("Nassau", "Suffolk", "Queens")
)]

county_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  county_plot_data,
  ggplot2::aes(x = factor(scenario_cluster), y = pct, fill = county)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.75) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Phase 9 County Composition by Scenario Cluster",
    subtitle = "Each bar sums to 100% within a scenario cluster",
    x = "Scenario cluster",
    y = "Share (%)",
    fill = "County"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )

print(county_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_county_composition.png"),
  plot = county_plot,
  width = 7.5,
  height = 4.8,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-cluster-fingerprint, fig.height=5.8, fig.width=8}
# v2.0: Add sev4_rate row; use final cluster names on x-axis
fingerprint_source <- merge(
  scenario_weather_time_profile[, .(
    scenario_cluster, night_pct, weekend_pct,
    junction_pct, traffic_signal_pct, crossing_pct,
    mean_visibility, mean_temperature, mean_wind_speed
  )],
  scenario_severity_profile[, .(scenario_cluster, sev34_pct, sev4_rate)],
  by = "scenario_cluster", all.x = TRUE
)

# Attach short final names for x-axis labels
fingerprint_source <- merge(
  fingerprint_source,
  scenario_final_names[, .(
    scenario_cluster,
    cluster_label = paste0(
      "S", scenario_cluster, "\n",
      substr(gsub(" ---.*", "", final_name), 1, 16)
    )
  )],
  by = "scenario_cluster", all.x = TRUE
)

fingerprint_long <- data.table::melt(
  fingerprint_source,
  id.vars       = c("scenario_cluster", "cluster_label"),
  variable.name = "metric",
  value.name    = "metric_value"
)

metric_labels <- c(
  night_pct          = "Night share (%)",
  weekend_pct        = "Weekend share (%)",
  junction_pct       = "Junction share (%)",
  traffic_signal_pct = "Signal share (%)",
  crossing_pct       = "Crossing share (%)",
  mean_visibility    = "Mean visibility (mi)",
  mean_temperature   = "Mean temperature (F)",
  mean_wind_speed    = "Mean wind speed (mph)",
  sev34_pct          = "Severity 3/4 share (%)",
  sev4_rate          = "Severity 4 rate (%)"
)

fingerprint_long[, metric_label := factor(
  metric_labels[as.character(metric)],
  levels = rev(metric_labels)
)]
fingerprint_long[, scaled_value := rescale01(metric_value), by = metric]

fingerprint_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
  fingerprint_long,
  ggplot2::aes(
    x    = reorder(cluster_label, scenario_cluster),
    y    = metric_label,
    fill = scaled_value
  )
) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(
    ggplot2::aes(label = round(metric_value, 1)),
    size = 3, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#f7fbff", mid = "#6baed6", high = "#08306b",
    midpoint = 0.5, na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title    = "Scenario Cluster Fingerprint Heatmap",
    subtitle = "Tile color scaled within each metric row; labels show actual values",
    x        = "Scenario cluster",
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = "Relative\nstrength"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid       = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    axis.text.x      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 8, lineheight = 1.1),
    axis.text.y      = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9)
  )

print(fingerprint_plot)

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "phase9_cluster_fingerprint_heatmap.png"),
  plot = fingerprint_plot,
  width = 8,
  height = 5.8,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r phase-9-export-artifacts}
write.csv(
  df_clustered,
  file.path(output_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

table_exports <- list(
  phase6_validation_indices = validation_table,
  phase6_validation_metadata = validation_metadata,
  phase6_stability_summary = stability_summary,
  phase6_selection_summary = selection_summary,
  phase7_eps_trials = eps_trials,
  phase7_parameter_summary = parameter_summary,
  phase7_dbscan_cluster_sizes = spatial_cluster_sizes,
  phase7_corridor_assignments = non_noise_assignments,
  phase8_severity_profile = scenario_severity_profile,
  phase8_county_profile = scenario_county_profile,
  phase8_weather_time_profile = scenario_weather_time_profile,
  phase8_spatial_scenario_crosstab = spatial_scenario_crosstab,
  phase8_spatial_scenario_dominance = spatial_scenario_dominance,
  phase8_scenario_final_names = scenario_final_names,
  phase8_spatial_final_names = spatial_final_names
)

if (!is.null(validation_repeat_results)) {
  table_exports$phase6_validation_repeats <- validation_repeat_results
}

if (!is.null(stability_repeat_results)) {
  table_exports$phase6_stability_repeats <- stability_repeat_results
}

invisible(lapply(names(table_exports), function(table_name) {
  fwrite(table_exports[[table_name]], file.path(table_dir, paste0(table_name, ".csv")))
}))

figure_exports <- c(
  "phase5_kproto_elbow_plot.png",
  "phase6_cluster_profiles.pdf",
  "phase7_knn_distance_plot.png",
  "phase7_spatial_hotspot_map.png",
  "phase7_spatial_density_heatmap.png",
  "phase9_validation_index_plot.png",
  "phase9_stability_plot.png",
  "phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap.png",
  "phase9_spatial_cluster_facets_heatmap.png",
  "phase9_scenario_risk_bubble.png",
  "phase9_county_composition.png",
  "phase9_cluster_fingerprint_heatmap.png"
)

invisible(lapply(figure_exports, function(file_name) {
  source_path <- file.path(output_dir, file_name)
  if (file.exists(source_path)) {
    file.copy(source_path, file.path(figure_dir, file_name), overwrite = TRUE)
  }
}))

file.copy(
  file.path(output_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv"),
  file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv"),
  overwrite = TRUE
)

export_manifest <- data.table(
  artifact_type = c(
    rep("figure", length(figure_exports)),
    rep("table", length(table_exports)),
    "table"
  ),
  artifact_name = c(
    figure_exports,
    paste0(names(table_exports), ".csv"),
    "li_accidents_clustered.csv"
  ),
  target_path = c(
    file.path(figure_dir, figure_exports),
    file.path(table_dir, paste0(names(table_exports), ".csv")),
    file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv")
  )
)

fwrite(export_manifest, file.path(output_dir, "phase9_export_manifest.csv"))

knitr::kable(export_manifest, caption = "Phase 9 export manifest")
```

```{r phase-9-feature-sensitivity-plot, fig.height=4.5, fig.width=7.5}
# v2.0: Side-by-side silhouette comparison — full vs reduced feature set.
# Shows whether dropping imputed features (Pressure, Visibility) improves quality.
if (file.exists(
  file.path(output_dir, "phase6_feature_silhouette_comparison.csv")
)) {
  feat_compare <- data.table::fread(
    file.path(output_dir, "phase6_feature_silhouette_comparison.csv")
  )

  feat_compare[, feature_set_label := ifelse(
    feature_set == "full",
    "Full (16 features)",
    "Reduced (14 features,\nno Pressure/Visibility)"
  )]

  feature_sensitivity_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    feat_compare,
    ggplot2::aes(
      x    = factor(k),
      y    = silhouette,
      fill = feature_set_label
    )
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.65) +
    ggplot2::geom_text(
      ggplot2::aes(label = round(silhouette, 3)),
      position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.65),
      vjust = -0.4, size = 3, fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    ggplot2::geom_hline(
      yintercept = 0.30, linetype = "dashed",
      color = "#e31a1c", linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    ggplot2::annotate(
      "text", x = Inf, y = 0.305,
      label = "Threshold 0.30",
      hjust = 1.05, size = 2.8, color = "#e31a1c"
    ) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "Full (16 features)"                        = "#4292c6",
        "Reduced (14 features,\nno Pressure/Visibility)" = "#e6550d"
      )
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title    = "Feature Sensitivity: Silhouette by k",
      subtitle = "Reduced set drops Pressure(in) and Visibility(mi) — both were median-imputed",
      x        = "Number of clusters (k)",
      y        = "Silhouette width",
      fill     = "Feature set"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title       = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      legend.position  = "bottom"
    )

  print(feature_sensitivity_plot)

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = file.path(
      output_dir, "figures", "phase9_feature_sensitivity.png"
    ),
    plot   = feature_sensitivity_plot,
    width  = 7.5, height = 4.5, dpi = 300
  )
} else {
  cat("Feature silhouette comparison not available — skipping plot.\n")
}
```

```{r phase-9-interactive-exports}
interactive_exports <- data.table(
  artifact_name = c(
    "phase9_validation_index_interactive.html",
    "phase9_stability_interactive.html",
    "phase9_spatial_hotspot_interactive.html",
    "phase9_spatial_density_interactive.html",
    "phase9_scenario_risk_interactive.html",
    "phase9_county_composition_interactive.html",
    "phase9_cluster_fingerprint_interactive.html",
    "phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap_interactive.html"
  ),
  target_path = file.path(
    interactive_dir,
    c(
      "phase9_validation_index_interactive.html",
      "phase9_stability_interactive.html",
      "phase9_spatial_hotspot_interactive.html",
      "phase9_spatial_density_interactive.html",
      "phase9_scenario_risk_interactive.html",
      "phase9_county_composition_interactive.html",
      "phase9_cluster_fingerprint_interactive.html",
      "phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap_interactive.html"
    )
  ),
  saved = FALSE,
  note = ifelse(
    interactive_exports_enabled,
    "Saved as interactive HTML",
    "Install plotly and htmlwidgets to enable interactive exports"
  )
)

if (interactive_exports_enabled) {
  spatial_interactive_data <- copy(df_spatial_plot)
  if (nrow(spatial_interactive_data) > 15000) {
    set.seed(42)
    spatial_interactive_data <- spatial_interactive_data[
      ,
      .SD[sample(.N, size = min(.N, max(1L, ceiling(15000 * .N / nrow(df_spatial_plot)))))],
      by = cluster_label
    ]
  }

  spatial_map_interactive <- ggplot2::ggplot(
    spatial_interactive_data,
    ggplot2::aes(x = x_km, y = y_km, color = cluster_label,
                 text = paste0(
                   "Cluster: ", cluster_label,
                   "<br>x_km: ", round(x_km, 2),
                   "<br>y_km: ", round(y_km, 2)
                 ))
  ) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Interactive Spatial Hotspot View",
      subtitle = "Rendered on a representative sample for smoother browsing",
      x = "Projected x (km)",
      y = "Projected y (km)",
      color = "Cluster"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal()

  interactive_widgets <- list(
    phase9_validation_index_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(validation_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y")),
    phase9_stability_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(stability_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill")),
    phase9_spatial_hotspot_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(spatial_map_interactive, tooltip = "text"),
    phase9_spatial_density_interactive.html = plotly::plot_ly(
      spatial_interactive_data,
      x = ~x_km,
      y = ~y_km,
      type = "histogram2d",
      colorscale = "Viridis",
      nbinsx = 80,
      nbinsy = 60
    ) |>
      plotly::layout(
        title = "Interactive Spatial Density Heatmap",
        xaxis = list(title = "Projected x (km)"),
        yaxis = list(title = "Projected y (km)")
      ),
    phase9_scenario_risk_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(risk_bubble_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "size", "colour")),
    phase9_county_composition_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(county_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill")),
    phase9_cluster_fingerprint_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(fingerprint_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill")),
    phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap_interactive.html = plotly::ggplotly(heatmap_plot, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill"))
  )

  invisible(lapply(names(interactive_widgets), function(file_name) {
    htmlwidgets::saveWidget(
      interactive_widgets[[file_name]],
      file = file.path(interactive_dir, file_name),
      selfcontained = FALSE
    )
  }))

  interactive_exports[, saved := file.exists(target_path)]
}

fwrite(interactive_exports, file.path(output_dir, "phase9_interactive_manifest.csv"))

knitr::kable(interactive_exports, caption = "Phase 9 interactive export manifest")
```

```{r phase-9-summary}
phase_9_summary <- data.table(
  checkpoint = c(
    "Figure directory created",
    "Table directory created",
    "Validation plot saved",
    "Stability plot saved",
    "Heatmap saved",
    "Spatial density heatmap saved",
    "Spatial cluster facet heatmap saved",
    "Scenario risk bubble saved",
    "County composition plot saved",
    "Cluster fingerprint heatmap saved",
    "Labeled CSV exported",
    "Interactive manifest saved",
    "Export manifest saved"
  ),
  status = c(
    dir.exists(figure_dir),
    dir.exists(table_dir),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_validation_index_plot.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_stability_plot.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_spatial_scenario_heatmap.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase7_spatial_density_heatmap.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_spatial_cluster_facets_heatmap.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_scenario_risk_bubble.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_county_composition.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_cluster_fingerprint_heatmap.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase9_interactive_manifest.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase9_export_manifest.csv"))
  )
)

fwrite(phase_9_summary, file.path(output_dir, "phase9_summary.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_9_summary, caption = "Phase 9 readiness summary")
```

# Phase 10: Final Report and Submission Checklist

This phase keeps a simple readiness tracker for the final report assets and submission handoff.

```{r phase-10-readiness}
phase_10_checklist <- data.table(
  deliverable = c(
    "Long Island SRS source",
    "Execution plan source",
    "Main workflow Rmd",
    "Cleaned dataset",
    "Clustered labeled dataset",
    "Figure export folder",
    "Table export folder",
    "Interactive export manifest"
  ),
  path = c(
    "Li_Clustering_SRS.tex",
    "Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex",
    "li_clustering.Rmd",
    input_path,
    file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv"),
    figure_dir,
    table_dir,
    file.path(output_dir, "phase9_interactive_manifest.csv")
  ),
  ready = c(
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_SRS.tex"),
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex"),
    file.exists("li_clustering.Rmd"),
    file.exists(input_path),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv")),
    dir.exists(figure_dir),
    dir.exists(table_dir),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase9_interactive_manifest.csv"))
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_checklist, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_readiness_checklist.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_checklist, caption = "Phase 10 readiness checklist")
```

```{r phase-10-report-outline}
phase_10_report_outline <- data.table(
  report_section = c(
    "Introduction and motivation",
    "Dataset description and audit findings",
    "Feature engineering decisions",
    "Method selection rationale",
    "Scenario clustering results",
    "Spatial hotspot results",
    "Interpretation and cross-tab findings",
    "Limitations",
    "Conclusion and safety priorities"
  ),
  main_source = c(
    "Li_Clustering_SRS.tex",
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_severity_profile.csv"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_weather_time_profile.csv"),
    "Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex",
    file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_validation_index_plot.png"),
    file.path(figure_dir, "phase7_spatial_hotspot_map.png"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_scenario_crosstab.csv"),
    file.path(output_dir, "phase10_limitations_table.csv"),
    file.path(output_dir, "phase10_research_question_coverage.csv")
  ),
  ready = c(
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_SRS.tex"),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_severity_profile.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_weather_time_profile.csv")),
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex"),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase9_validation_index_plot.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(figure_dir, "phase7_spatial_hotspot_map.png")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_scenario_crosstab.csv")),
    TRUE,
    TRUE
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_report_outline, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_report_outline.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_report_outline, caption = "Phase 10 report outline and source map")
```

```{r phase-10-limitations}
# v2.0: Cluster quality failure rows prepended FIRST so they appear at the
# top of the rendered table and cannot be missed in the final report.
cluster_quality_rows <- data.table(
  limitation = c(
    "Scenario cluster quality below standard thresholds",
    "Scenario clustering is exploratory only"
  ),
  report_language = c(
    paste0(
      "Silhouette width = ", round(selected_silhouette, 3),
      " (published threshold >= 0.30; Kaufman and Rousseeuw 1990). ",
      "Jaccard stability index = ", round(selected_jaccard_v2, 3),
      " (threshold >= 0.40 for exploratory, >= 0.60 for acceptable; ",
      "Hennig 2007). Both metrics fall below acceptable thresholds, ",
      "indicating that cluster boundaries are weak and that bootstrap ",
      "resamples do not reliably reproduce the same partition."
    ),
    paste0(
      "Because silhouette and Jaccard are both below threshold, ",
      "the k = ", selected_k, " scenario clustering solution should be ",
      "interpreted as a set of candidate patterns for hypothesis generation, ",
      "not as stable or confirmed accident archetypes. ",
      "The elevation of Severity 3/4 in Clusters 4 and 5 is a plausible ",
      "signal but requires replication on a new sample before it can be ",
      "treated as a reliable finding. ",
      "DBSCAN spatial results are separately validated and remain defensible."
    )
  ),
  include_in_final_report = TRUE
)

phase_10_limitations <- rbind(
  cluster_quality_rows,
  data.table(
    limitation = c(
      "Duration capping",
      "Precipitation exclusion",
      "Distance exclusion",
      "Severity 2 dominance",
      "Sampled Phase 6 validation",
      "DBSCAN parameter sensitivity"
    ),
    report_language = c(
      "Duration was capped at the 99th percentile to reduce the effect of extreme outliers, so the top 1% of incidents lose some real variation.",
      "Precipitation was excluded from the core model because missingness was high enough (20.1%) to make routine imputation less defensible.",
      "Distance(mi) was excluded because the large share of zero values suggests a reporting artifact rather than a stable behavioral feature.",
      "Severity 2 dominates the dataset (85% of rows), so subtle differences among severe crashes can be harder to separate.",
      if (run_profile == "full_srs") {
        "Phase 6 is configured for full-data validation on a stronger machine."
      } else if (run_profile == "consensus_16gb") {
        paste(
          "Phase 6 uses repeated sampled validation (4 x 4,000 rows) and",
          "repeated stability checks (3 x 2,500 rows, 10 bootstraps each)",
          "to build a consensus k on a 16 GB machine."
        )
      } else {
        "Phase 6 uses single-sample silhouette (5,000 rows) and stability (3,000 rows) checks; full-data version archived for high-memory rerun."
      },
      "Spatial hotspot shapes can shift if eps or minPts changes; corridor labels should be read as data-driven approximations confirmed by centroid inspection."
    ),
    include_in_final_report = TRUE
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_limitations, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_limitations_table.csv"))

knitr::kable(
  phase_10_limitations,
  caption = paste(
    "Phase 10 limitations table (v2.0).",
    "First two rows document cluster quality failure explicitly."
  )
)
```

```{r phase-10-research-question-coverage}
phase_10_rq_coverage <- data.table(
  research_focus = c(
    "Scenario accident archetypes",
    "Spatial hotspot corridors",
    "High-risk severity patterns",
    "County composition differences",
    "Hotspot-to-scenario linkage"
  ),
  evidence_source = c(
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_scenario_final_names.csv"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_final_names.csv"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_severity_profile.csv"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_county_profile.csv"),
    file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_scenario_crosstab.csv")
  ),
  ready = c(
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_scenario_final_names.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_final_names.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_severity_profile.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_county_profile.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "phase8_spatial_scenario_crosstab.csv"))
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_rq_coverage, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_research_question_coverage.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_rq_coverage, caption = "Phase 10 research-question coverage")
```

```{r phase-10-submission-manifest}
phase_10_submission_manifest <- data.table(
  deliverable_group = c(
    "Core source",
    "Core source",
    "Core source",
    "Data",
    "Data",
    "Exports",
    "Exports",
    "Exports"
  ),
  deliverable = c(
    "Li_Clustering_SRS.tex",
    "Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex",
    "li_clustering.Rmd",
    "li_accidents_clean.csv",
    "li_accidents_clustered.csv",
    "Figure directory",
    "Table directory",
    "Phase 10 checklist bundle"
  ),
  path = c(
    "Li_Clustering_SRS.tex",
    "Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex",
    "li_clustering.Rmd",
    input_path,
    file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv"),
    figure_dir,
    table_dir,
    output_dir
  ),
  ready = c(
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_SRS.tex"),
    file.exists("Li_Clustering_Execution_Plan.tex"),
    file.exists("li_clustering.Rmd"),
    file.exists(input_path),
    file.exists(file.path(table_dir, "li_accidents_clustered.csv")),
    dir.exists(figure_dir),
    dir.exists(table_dir),
    dir.exists(output_dir)
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_submission_manifest, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_submission_manifest.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_submission_manifest, caption = "Phase 10 submission manifest")
```

```{r phase-10-summary}
phase_10_summary <- data.table(
  checkpoint = c(
    "Readiness checklist saved",
    "Report outline saved",
    "Limitations table saved",
    "Research-question coverage saved",
    "Submission manifest saved"
  ),
  status = c(
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_readiness_checklist.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_report_outline.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_limitations_table.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_research_question_coverage.csv")),
    file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_submission_manifest.csv"))
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_summary, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_summary.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_summary, caption = "Phase 10 readiness summary")
```

```{r phase-10-sanity-audit}
selected_jaccard_value <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(selected_jaccard))

phase_10_sanity_audit <- data.table(
  check = c(
    "Scenario names are unique",
    "Scenario labels have no gaps",
    "Spatial labels have no gaps",
    "Selected scenario stability is at least moderate",
    "Largest spatial cluster stays below 60% of all rows",
    "DBSCAN cluster-count target met",
    "DBSCAN noise target met"
  ),
  status = c(
    uniqueN(scenario_final_names$final_name) == nrow(scenario_final_names),
    label_join_check[metric == "Scenario label NAs", value][[1]] == 0,
    label_join_check[metric == "Spatial label NAs", value][[1]] == 0,
    !is.na(selected_jaccard_value) && selected_jaccard_value >= 0.4,
    largest_non_noise_share_pct <= phase7_max_cluster_share_cap,
    uniqueN(df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster[df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster > 0]) >= 3 &
      uniqueN(df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster[df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster > 0]) <= 15,
    round(100 * mean(df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster == 0), 2) >= 5 &
      round(100 * mean(df_spatial_clusters$spatial_cluster == 0), 2) <= 30
  ),
  note = c(
    "Final scenario names should be distinct for presentation and discussion.",
    "Every row should carry a scenario label after the join.",
    "Every row should carry a spatial label after the join.",
    "A Jaccard score below 0.4 should be presented as exploratory rather than settled.",
    "This avoids one hotspot absorbing most of Long Island.",
    "The SRS target is between 3 and 15 non-noise clusters.",
    "The SRS target is between 5% and 30% noise."
  )
)

fwrite(phase_10_sanity_audit, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_sanity_audit.csv"))

phase_10_summary <- rbind(
  phase_10_summary,
  data.table(
    checkpoint = "Sanity audit saved",
    status = file.exists(file.path(output_dir, "phase10_sanity_audit.csv"))
  )
)
fwrite(phase_10_summary, file.path(output_dir, "phase10_summary.csv"))

knitr::kable(phase_10_sanity_audit, caption = "Phase 10 sanity audit")
```

# Notes for the next phases

- Phase 2 should stay read-only and focus on profiling the cleaned dataset.
- Phase 3 now derives the main engineered features needed for the scenario and spatial matrices.
- Phase 4 now defines the actual scenario and spatial matrices for clustering.
- Phase 5 now sets up the candidate k-prototypes runs and their core diagnostics.
- Phase 6 now validates the candidate solutions, checks stability, and assigns a selected k.
- Phase 7 now sets DBSCAN parameters, creates the spatial hotspot solution, and assigns corridor-style labels.
- Phase 8 now joins cluster labels back to the working data and builds the interpretation tables.
- Phase 9 now exports the report-ready figures, tables, and labeled dataset.
- Phase 10 now tracks final report and submission readiness.
- `run_profile = "consensus_16gb"` is the current default in this file.
- `run_profile = "local_safe"` is the quickest laptop-friendly option.
- `run_profile = "consensus_16gb"` repeats sampled Phase 6 checks and aggregates them into a consensus choice for 16 GB machines.
- `run_profile = "full_srs"` is reserved for a stronger machine that can support the full Phase 6 diagnostics.
- The current default uses repeated sampled validation and repeated sampled stability checks to stay within 16 GB memory limits.
- Interactive HTML exports are optional and now turn on automatically when the needed packages are available.
- The original full-data Phase 6 validation and stability calls are archived in `eval=FALSE` chunks for later reruns.
- Phase 3 and later should write tables, figures, and exports into `local_artifacts/output/`.
- No `.RData` workspace files are needed for this workflow.

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
